_comment: Public template-lineage table. Fork the ROOT, not a descendant that carries a peer core's phantom/dead clock groups. IMPORTANT public finding: open-fpga/core-template ships NO license file (default all-rights-reserved) -- the porter must clone it themselves; it cannot be redistributed. All commit hashes and repo names are public.

templates.note: The lineage root: the official Analogue openFPGA template (v1.0.0, 2022-07-29). Every corpus port inherits its skeleton. Fork this, not a descendant. Ships NO LICENSE (all-rights-reserved) -- clone it yourself; do not redistribute. The blanket set_clock_groups -asynchronous cut originates in its src/fpga/core/core_constraints.sdc.
templates.provenance: public repo open-fpga/core-template; commit ad20a21 = v1.0.0; no LICENSE/COPYING present at any tagged commit (public, verifiable).

templates.note: opengateware's Gateman framework re-emits the BSP (platform/pocket/bsp/pocket/sys_constr.sdc). Its newest cores (irem_m72, irem_m92, computer-msx) carry Gateman-generated constraints -- a descendant lineage; prefer the root unless you specifically want the Gateman BSP conventions.
templates.provenance: public opengateware cores; sys_constr.sdc header 'Generated by OpenGateware - Gateman CLI v0.1.0'.

templates.note: agg23's console line uses a single mf_pllbase PLL with multiple outputs; a template-verbatim descendant. A good reference for console (SDRAM-backed) ports, but fork the root and adopt the mf_pllbase pattern rather than forking a specific game.
templates.provenance: public agg23/openfpga-* repos; copied-in core_constraints.sdc, skeleton unchanged.
